tiktok.caption: Your 401k match is already in your account. Quit too soon and your company takes it all back — legally. Most workers have no idea.

youtube_short.title: Quitting Your Job Could Cost You $14,000
title_alternatives: Your 401k Match Isn't Yours Yet — Here's Why
The 401k Vesting Trap Nobody Warns You About
youtube_short.description: Most people assume their employer's 401k match belongs to them the moment it hits their account — but vesting schedules mean your company can legally take it all back if you quit too soon. 57% of plans use vesting schedules, and 1 in 3 workers leaves before they're fully vested, forfeiting an average of $14,000. Before your next job change, look up your vest date — waiting even a few extra months could change everything. Follow for more money traps hiding in plain sight. #401k #personalfinance #retirement #investing #moneytips

hook_line: Your company can take back your 401k match
